title: Control-M Agent Availability and Heartbeat Gap Detection
dataSources: `index=batch` `sourcetype=controlm:agent` heartbeat, status, and disconnect events from Agent-to-Server tunnel
app: Control-M/Agent diagnostic forwarder, syslog, or Automation API poller
spl:
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:agent"
| eval host=coalesce(agent_host, node_id, host)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(status) as agent_status, values(message) as msgs by host
| eval gap_sec=now()-last_seen
| where gap_sec > 900 OR match(lower(agent_status),"(?i)down|disconnected|unavailable")
| sort -gap_sec
description: Silent agent loss leaves jobs unrunnable on remote hosts while the schedule still assumes capacity exists. Heartbeat gaps are the fastest signal before a mountain of late jobs appears in the Enterprise Manager.
value: Reduces batch outage duration on distributed platforms and proves agent resiliency for audit questions.
implementation: Normalize timestamps to UTC; agents behind NAT may batch heartbeats—tune gap_sec per region. Enrich with CMDB owner and escalation tier. Pair with network device logs for path failures.
visualization: Choropleth or location map of agents, single-value (agents in gap), timeline of disconnect reasons.
grandmaExplanation: We watch batch and control jobs so missed handoffs or resource limits do not break business processing.
knownFalsePositives: Transient errors during platform maintenance, network blips, or certificate renewals on shared services.
